rulings.Case Title: PEGASTAFF, A DIVISION OF PEGASOFT CORPORATION, A VS. CORESTAFF SERVICES, LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Limit Defendant'S Subpoenas For The Production Of Business Records; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PEGASTAFF's Motion To Quash Limit Defendant'S Subpoenas For The Production Of Business Records. Hearing required. It is evident from both supplemental papers and the additional discovery motions that significant differences exist between the parties. The court is inclined to appoint a discovery referee. The parties should meet and confer in an effort to agree on a referee, but if they are unable to do so, each side should identify up to three prospective referees. The parties should also be prepared to discuss the new trial date. The current trial date of November 26, 2012 remains in effect because the agreed upon trial date of April 1, 2013 is a court holiday. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ALLEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mary Allen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 3, Plaintiff Mary Allen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Shapiro as follows: Granted. No attorneys' fees awarded. Defendant City and County of San Francisco must answer the disputed questions with full, complete, and substantive answers within 15 days. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling The Pltf'S To Provide Futther Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Three And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2040.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling The Pltf'S To Provide Futther Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Three And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2040.00 Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE E. LOPEZ VS. VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANTs VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS, LP, VCA ANTECH and ALAN STEWART's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend as to the allegations seeking punitive damages under Civil Code 3294 and general (emotional distress, pain and suffering) damages, and denied as to the allegations seeking exemplary damages under Civil Code 3340. Plaintiff does not allege malicious, oppressive, or fraudulent conduct, nor does it appear that she is able to do so in good faith. General (emotional distress, pain and suffering) damages is barred per McMahon v. Craig (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 222.. The allegations seeking Civil Code 3340 exemplary damages are sufficient because the second amended complaint alleges grossly negligent conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendants are required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of their counsel in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE E. LOPEZ VS. VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANTs VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS, LP, VCA ANTECH and ALAN STEWART's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Paragraphs 26 and 72-92 alleges sufficient facts to support a claim for breach of fiduciary duty. The court overrules the demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action. Paragraphs 98 and 102-103 alleges sufficient facts that defendant's representations were unreasonable and thus plaintiff has stated a cognizable claim for negligent misrepresentation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SEAN DEVRIES VS. APPLE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT APPLE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not alleged, nor does it appear that he will be able to allege, that Siri's responses to any questions posed to it, viewed in light of the advertising done by Apple for its products which includes Siri, are likely to deceive a reasonable consumer. Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GWEN FEHER, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL VS. GEMMITI MODEL ART et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 15, PLAINTIFF GWEN FEHER, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL others similarly situated's DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: JOHN DUDLEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 23, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. The Complaint is barred by the "home rule" doctrine. (See Dimon v. County of Los Angeles, (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1276; Curcini v. County of Alameda (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 629.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
